Source code for killMS.Other.rad2hmsdms

#!/usr/bin/env python
"""
killMS, a package for calibration in radio interferometry.

SKA South Africa, Rhodes University

of the License, or (at your option) any later version.

but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
GNU General Public License for more details.

You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.
"""
from __future__ import absolute_import
from __future__ import division
from __future__ import print_function

import numpy as np



[docs]
def rad2hmsdms(rad:float,
               Type:str="dec",
               deg:bool=False) -> str:
    '''
    Converts radians to SkyCoord.to_string('hmsdms') equivalent.
    Deprecated, should be replaced with astropy function and units.
    Returns HMS string.

    :param rad: Input angle in radians.
    :type rad: float
    :param Type: Checks if declination or hour angle (RA). Converts properly.
    :type Type: str
    :param deg: Option to take in deg and convert to rad.
    :type deg: bool
    '''
    if deg==False:
        deg=rad*180/np.pi
    else:
        deg=rad

    strsgn="+"
    if Type=="ra":
        deg/=15.
        strsgn=""

    if np.sign(deg)==-1.: strsgn="-"
    deg=np.abs(deg)
    degd=int(deg)
    degms=(deg-degd)*60.
    degm=int(degms)
    degs=((degms-degm)*60)
    return "%s%2.2i %2.2i %06.3f"%(strsgn,degd,degm,degs)

killMS
